title: [InstCombine] fold `gepi _, (srem x, y)` to `gepi _, (urem x, y)` if `y` is power-of-2
author: imkiva
description:
This PR adds a small, targeted InstCombine fold for the pattern:
```
%idx = srem i64 %x, 2^k
%p   = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %base, i64 %idx
```

When the GEP is inbounds + nuw, and the divisor is a non-zero power-of-two constant, the signed remainder cannot produce a negative offset without violating the inbounds/nuw constraints. In that case we can canonicalize the index to a non-negative form and expose the common power-of-two rewrite:

- Rewrite the GEP index from `srem %x, 2^k` to `urem %x, 2^k`
- Create a new GEP with the new index and replace the original GEP
- the `urem %x, 2^k` will further folds to `and %x (2^k-1)`

resulting the following pattern

```
%idx = and i64 %x, (2^k-1)
%p   = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %base, i64 %idx
```

Fixes #180097.
generalized alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/8EBxug

tests.test_body:
declare void @use(i8)

define ptr @neg_multi_use(ptr %foo, i64 %x) {
  %idx = srem i64 %x, 4
  call void @use(i64 %idx)
  %p = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %foo, i64 %idx
  ret ptr %p
}

comments.author: imkiva
comments.body:
> Generalized proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/8EBxug

Thank you for the generalized proof. It seems inbounds + nuw + positive power-of-2 index is enough to prove to correctness.

```
%ctpop = call i8 @llvm.ctpop(i8 %C)
%pow2 = icmp eq i8 %ctpop, 1
```

But the ctpop here doesn't check `%C` is positive --- Can we relax the condition to all power-of-2 index? I am confused why negative indices can still be "inbounds".

https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/BW5TpE

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> Can we relax the condition to all power-of-2 index? 

Yes.

> I am confused why negative indices can still be "inbounds".

Negative indices can be inbounds (if the pointer doesn't point to the base of the object) but they can't be inbounds and nuw at the same time. getelementptr returns poison in that case, and we can replace the poison with any other value.

comments.author: imkiva
comments.body: I replaced the pattern matching on index from `m_ConstantInt` to `m_Value`, and used `isKnownToBeAPowerOfTwo` to check if we can transform the srem to urem, according to the generalized proof.

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body: @imkiva Sorry I forgot you have the commit access...

comments.author: nagisa
comments.body:
> Looks like we can always convert srem x, pow2 to urem if we can prove the result is non-negative

Correct, but I could not come up with any other sequence of instructions where this sort of proof would come up naturally without assumes. I tried various `nuw` tagged arithmetics for example and alive wasn't happy with it. gepi was my direct use-case so I went with that. If there's a more general way to match the same requirements, it would be even better ig.
